creator: jamesm.sitegen@gmail.com
text:
If you goto http://people.freebsd.org/~nik/advocacy/myths.html#old-codebase you would notice the following..

Architecture ports: Ports of *BSD are under way or already exist on many other architectures, including SPARC, Alpha, and PPC. NetBSD and OpenBSD both have more architecture ports than FreeBSD, which is, at the time of writing, running on i386, Alpha and SPARC.

I'm sure FBSD supports more than that now :P

Fix: 

Update the small list..
How-To-Repeat: Goto http://people.freebsd.org/~nik/advocacy/myths.html#old-codebase
